summary:
Crowded teeth and crossbites do not result specifically due to tongue thrusting based on available literature; other factors may also influence such outcomes. Spacing between front teeth and changes in jaw alignment are more commonly associated with it according to reviewed research articles focusing on its impacts.
To refine for clarity while maintaining brevity:

Crowding or crossbite issues typically aren't linked uniquely to tongue thrusting as per existing medical reviews which highlight spaced-front-teeth and misalignment problems instead. Thus, they stand out among listed options as non-characteristic symptoms related only by tongue thrust habit. To summarize concisely yet comprehensively within two sentences focused clinically remains challenging given limited direct references but aligns logically hereafter presented rationale.

Spacing between front teeth and changes in jaw alignment are more commonly associated with it according to reviewed research articles focusing on its impacts.
To refine for clarity while maintaining brevity:

Crowding or crossbite issues typically aren't linked uniquely to tongue thrusting as per existing medical reviews which highlight spaced-front-teeth and misalignment problems instead. Thus, they stand out among listed options as non-characteristic symptoms related only by tongue thrust habit. To summarize concisely yet comprehensively within two sentences focused clinically remains challenging given limited direct references but aligns logically hereafter presented rationale.
